_comment: Copy this file to deal-playbook.local.json and fill in your real negotiating positions. The .local.json is gitignored and never committed. This template is the shape only, with all positions null. Every contract atom reads the playbook first; if it is still this null template, the atom runs in a clearly labeled provisional mode against the generic creator-side defaults in shared/contract-engine.md, never against invented positions.
_model: Four-tier clause library. For each clause: standard = the position you open with; fallbacks = ordered concessions you can live with; never = lines you will not cross; the_one_thing = the single point that matters most on this clause. Leave a tier null or empty until you have decided it. Do not invent positions.

usage_licensing_rights._covers: where, how long, and by whom sponsored content may be reused; organic vs paid; license duration

exclusivity._covers: category scope, competitor list, platforms, and duration of any no-competing-brand window

deliverables_and_revisions._covers: format and count of deliverables, number of revision rounds, and the approval window

payment_terms_and_kill_fee._covers: fee, payment timing (for example net 30 from delivery), deposit, and kill fee if the brand cancels

ftc_disclosure._covers: who is responsible for the #ad / paid-partnership disclosure and confirmation the contract does not try to waive it

content_approval_and_veto._covers: brand review and approval rights, veto scope, and the turnaround window for approvals

whitelisting_and_paid_boosting._covers: whether the brand may run your content as paid ads (whitelisting / boosting) and on what terms

morality_clause._covers: conduct clauses that let the brand terminate; scope and mutuality

territory._covers: geographic scope of the license and exclusivity
